item.title: Need to handle kernel memory leak before main thread exits
item.body:

comments.body:
```
$ OSDK_LOCAL_DEV=1 MIRIFLAGS=-Zmiri-disable-leak-backtraces cargo osdk miri test

...
error: memory leaked: alloc480791 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc607728 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc734665 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc93586 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc220523 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc347460 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc474397 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc601334 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc728271 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

error: memory leaked: alloc2319452 (Kernel, size: 64, align: 64)

note: set `MIRIFLAGS=-Zmiri-ignore-leaks` to disable this check

error: aborting due to 37499 previous errors; 6 warnings emitted
```

We should handle the leak for different alloation kinds:
* Kernel alloc kind currently is from `lazy_alloc_typed_slot_allocation`: ~they should be `may_leak=true`, meaning they are not reported as leak even if `memory.alloc_map` has them~
  * but we should free the kernel memory from interpreter, and manually remove kernel allocs from alloc_map, because the kernel has run to the end (maybe we can clearly rename the memory kind Kernel to KernelTypedSlot or something?)
* non-Kernel kind memory remains leaked as listed below
  * they are stack allocations, and all from `kernel_task_entry` 
  * probably related to https://github.com/KMiri-rs/KMiri/issues/100

